title: Grant IAM at the lowest node in the resource hierarchy
description: Bind predefined roles to a principal at the project/dataset/bucket level rather than the organization, respecting policy inheritance.
body: Use when granting GCP access or reviewing an allow policy. Do not bind roles at the organization or folder when a single project or resource suffices, and avoid primitive roles (owner/editor/viewer). Actions: pick a predefined role that matches the need, bind it at the lowest resource node (bucket, dataset, project) since IAM inherits downward, use conditions for time/resource constraints, and review effective access with the policy analyzer. Validation signals include the analyzer showing the principal's roles only at the intended scope and a denied attempt on a sibling resource. Risk boundary: any binding at org/folder scope or any primitive role grant is human-gated.
